data.title: Google, Sea Strike Deal to Develop Agentic AI Tools
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

Alphabet's Google and Southeast Asian consumer internet giant Sea are deepening ties to advance artificial-intelligence tools across the Singapore-based company's digital entertainment, e-commerce and financial services businesses.

Part of the agreement will include jointly building "an AI agentic shopping prototype" to integrate across Google and Sea's e-commerce platform, Shopee, the companies said Thursday. Both tech companies, which also offer digital payment services, plan to later trial an agentic payment experience across their platforms.

Sea's gaming unit, Garena, will use Google's AI solutions to improve gamer experiences and the productivity of game development and operations.

"AI is the next big technology revolution, and we believe that it has huge potential to positively transform our business and create value in our markets," Sea Chairman and Chief Executive Forrest Li said. "This partnership with Google on AI will drive innovation in the business application of the technology at scale."

The move builds on an existing partnership between New York-listed Sea and Google, which includes a recent collaboration between the YouTube Shopping affiliate program and Shopee.

According to a report by consultancy firm Momentum Works, Shopee was the dominant e-commerce platform in Southeast Asia, holding a 52% regional market share in 2024.

Part of Sea's strategy has included using AI and social commerce to enhance sellers' marketing strategies and provide tools that improve product recommendations for customers.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: EssilorLuxottica Shouldn't Be Spooked by Apple's AI Specs — Market Talk
data.description: An Apple sortie into smartglasses should be seen as a boon for EssilorLuxottica and not a threat to its position in the booming category, argues Cedric Rossi, an analyst at Stifel. Shares in the Franco-Italian eyewear giant took a hit this week on the back of a media report that Apple is gearing up to begin production of its own model of AI-powered glasses as early as the end of this year, potentially creating a rival to EssilorLuxottica's models in partnership with tech giant Meta. But an entry by Apple and other tech firms into the market should "accelerate consumer awareness and expand the total addressable market," Rossi says. EssilorLuxottica, meanwhile, retains several key advantages, notably its huge distribution network, he says. These "position the group to continue leading the smart eyewear category over the medium term," Rossi says. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

data.title: ZAWYA-PRESSR: The Authority of Social Contribution – Ma’an and Waha Capital sign agreement
data.description:
• Waha Capital has committed to contributing AED 6 Million to the Authority of Social Contribution – Ma’an which will direct the funds toward key projects that improve the quality of life for community members in Abu Dhabi
  • The Authority deploys contributions across a range of initiatives across the capital that address societal priorities and encourage community collaboration

Abu Dhabi: The Authority of Social Contribution – Ma’an, Abu Dhabi Government’s official channel to receive social contributions has signed an  agreement with Waha Capital, the Abu Dhabi‑listed investment management company, to jointly advance a variety of social projects in Abu Dhabi.

The agreement was signed by His Excellency Eng. Abdullah Humaid Al Ameri, Director General of the Authority of Social Contribution – Ma’an and Mohamed Hussain Al Nowais, Managing Director, Waha Capital.

The agreement comes as part of both parties’ commitment to support social priorities in key sectors including education, health, environment, infrastructure, and social services.

Priority projects include the social, health, wellness, humanitarian and environmental sectors with projects aligning with shared values and goals of both entities.

H.E. Eng. Abdullah Humaid Al Ameri, Director General of the Authority of Social Contribution – Ma’an, said: “Partnerships are at the heart of our work at the Authority of Social Contribution – Ma’an. Through this collaboration we will utilise the generous contribution to empower partners to lead and deliver major programmes and initiatives that address social priorities. We will shine a light on the impact partnerships can achieve in amplifying social impact, promoting community development and building a cooperative society.”

Mohamed Hussain Al Nowais, Managing Director, Waha Capital, said: “Our partnership with the Authority of Social Contribution - Ma’an reflects our commitment to supporting Abu Dhabi’s social development priorities and creating meaningful, lasting impact. At Waha Capital, we believe long-term value extends beyond financial performance. Through this collaboration, we are proud to contribute to initiatives that strengthen communities and support sustainable, positive change across the Emirate.”

Underscoring the Authority’s commitment to promoting community cohesion, the collaboration saw its first programme take place with volunteers distributing ‘Meer boxes’ containing Ramadan essentials to residents in ADNEC.

Volunteering projects enable the Authority to further its objectives to foster the values of shared responsibility and community giving among community members while encouraging them to play an active role in building a collaborative society.

To contribute to the Authority of Social Contribution – Ma’an, whether through monetary funds or volunteering time, please visit Ma'an, the Social Investment Fund of Abu Dhabi for more information.

About Authority of Social Contribution - Ma’an

Established in 2019 by the Department of Community Development Abu Dhabi (DCD), The Authority of Social Contribution – Ma’an is the Abu Dhabi government’s official channel to receive social contributions, dedicated to uniting community efforts and fostering a culture of giving by collecting contributions, directing them towards social priorities, empowering social enterprises, and promoting volunteering to build a cohesive community.

The Authority supports projects that address social priorities in health, education, environment, infrastructure, and social services, aiming to nurture a collaborative and active community by connecting individuals and entities in the public, private, and civil society spheres to support their communities.

Contributions made to the Authority of Social Contribution - Ma’an are transparently deployed in full to social projects led by key partners meaning benefactors can maximise the impact their funds have in driving community engagement and providing access to essential resources, programmes, and funding for organisations across Abu Dhabi to achieve their Corporate Social Responsibility and sustainable development goals.

Send us your press releases to pressrelease.zawya@lseg.com

data.title: Italy Current Account Surplus Widens
data.description:
Italy posted a current account surplus of €3.1 billion in December 2025, expanding from a €2.5 billion surplus in the corresponding month of the previous year.

The goods account surplus increased to €6.0 billion from €5.3 billion last year, and the primary income surplus rose to €1.5 billion from €0.8 billion.

These improvements more than offset wider deficits in services (€-2.0 billion vs €-1.3 billion in December 2025) and secondary income (€-2.5 billion vs €-2.3 billion).

For the full year of 2025, Italy’s current account surplus widened to €27.4 billion from €23.8 billion in 2024.

data.title: Rwanda central bank hikes key rate after inflation rises beyond target band
data.description:
Rwanda's central bank hiked its key lending rate by 50 basis points to 7.25% (RWREPO=ECI) on Thursday after inflation rose beyond its target band.

Inflation accelerated to 8.9% year on year in January (RWCPIY=ECI) from 8.0% in December.

The central bank aims to keep inflation in a range of 2% to 8%.

"The decision ... is a measured step we are taking to bring inflation back within the target band ... which is a necessary condition to sustain our economic growth," Governor Soraya Hakuziyaremye told a press conference.

data.title: Philippine Central Bank Delivers Another Rate Cut as Economy Slows — Update
data.description:
By Amanda Lee

The Philippine central bank cut rates at its first meeting of the year, a widely expected move as weak growth underlines the need for more economic support.

Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas lowered its benchmark overnight reverse repurchase rate by 25 basis points to 4.25% from 4.50% on Thursday, delivering a sixth straight round of easing. It reduced its benchmark lending rate to 4.75% from 5.00%.

The decision came as other central banks wind down their rate-cutting cycles, but persistently weak growth in the Philippines has kept cuts firmly on the table for the BSP. An added headwind from a graft scandal has widened the scope to loosen policy settings even further.

The central bank, which has now cut rates 225 basis points since its current easing cycle began in August 2024, has been monitoring the fallout from the probe into the alleged misuse of public funds.

In 2025, the Philippine economy grew at its weakest pace in five years, reflecting the impact of the controversy, which has delayed infrastructure spending and dented sentiment among businesses and investors.

Economic growth has undershot the central bank's expectations, it said Thursday, and recovery hinges largely on how quickly confidence recovers.

Against that backdrop, the February rate cut had been correctly predicted by 11 of the 12 economists polled by The Wall Street Journal.

While recent economic indicators point to a recovery at the start of 2026, the effects of the corruption scandal will continue to be felt over the coming quarters, said Jason Tuvey at Capital Economics. The research firm thinks that gross domestic product growth will come in at a below-consensus 4.5% this year.

A further loss of economic momentum could mean more rate cuts ahead, but the BSP's governor, Eli Remolona, said any decision will remain data-dependent.

The monetary board has always been guided by its primary mandate of keeping inflation low, supporting balanced and sustainable growth, he said at a press conference.

The central bank has slightly raised its inflation forecast, but expects price pressures to remain manageable.

What seems more complicated to gauge is the impact of the graft probe.

Remolona said the incident was a "shock" that had muddied the monetary policy outlook.

"Now we are less certain of where we want to go, where we would go, and it depends a lot more on the data that comes out. So the forward guidance is less clear now than it was," he said.

Asked if the BSP is in wait-and-see mode, Remolona said "absolutely." He also sounded somewhat upbeat about Thursday's move, despite the uncertainty.

"Our decision today may actually help to restore confidence, boosting investment and consumption," he said.

Write to Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com

data.title: Foreign holdings of Italian debt grow again in December, at slower pace
data.description:
Foreign investors' holdings of Italian government bonds rose again in December, the national central bank said on Thursday,

albeit at a slower pace compared to the previous month.

The exposure of foreign investors increased by 2.2 billion euros ($2.60 billion) after a 9.5 billion euro growth in November, the monthly balance of payments publication by the Bank of Italy showed.

($1 = 0.8475 euros)

data.title: Capita secures £137m pensions contract renewal
data.description:
It said the renewal, effective from the first quarter of this year, "deepens and extends the client relationship as Capita continues to deploy new technology and innovations to streamline transactions, expand capacity, and enhance customer experience".

Chris Clements, managing director of Capita Pension Solutions, said: "We are delighted to continue to strengthen this relationship, and are looking forward to continuing to deliver an exceptional service, with new technology solutions and tools to improve the customer experience and create better outcomes for all."

data.title: CoinDCX initiates ₹111 crore ESOP buyback for employees
data.description:
CoinDCX, an Indian cryptocurrency exchange, has announced an Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) buyback worth ₹111 crore. The buyback will cover more than 500 current and former employees.

The move is aimed at providing liquidity for employees who hold stock options in the company. CoinDCX said the buyback includes former employees in recognition of their contributions to the company’s growth.

Sumit Gupta, Co-founder of CoinDCX, highlighted that the buyback is intended to provide tangible value to employees and reflects the company’s approach to ownership-driven culture.

Founded in 2018 in Mumbai, CoinDCX has expanded its user base to over 2 crore across India and the UAE. The company has also extended its operations internationally through the acquisition of Dubai-based BitOasis in the MENA region, which later expanded into Bahrain in 2025.

The ESOP buyback marks one of the largest liquidity events by a cryptocurrency exchange in India. Analysts say such moves can help retain talent and provide employees with financial returns on their long-term contributions.

CoinDCX operates under the DCX Group, which encompasses multiple entities across India and international markets, including ventures in Web3 and digital wallets. The group is backed by global investors such as Pantera, Steadview Capital, Polychain Capital, B Capital Group, Bain Capital Ventures, and Coinbase Ventures.

data.title: Oil Pushes Above $70 as Investors Fear U.S. Strike on Iran
data.description:
By Dow Jones Newswires Staff

Oil pushed above $70 a barrel on rising concerns that a U.S. strike against Iran could be imminent. The threat of military action and subsequent disruption to oil trade and shipping also helped to push gold back above $5,000 an ounce.

Developments in the region damped sentiment even as U.S. equities benefited as fears from artificial-intelligence disruption abated on Wednesday. U.S. futures hovered around par in early European trade. A tech recovery in the U.S. spurred a rally in Korean and Japanese equities, though European stocks were down at the open after a suite of indexes hit record highs Wednesday.

Meanwhile, the release of minutes from the Federal Reserve's January rate-setting meeting on Wednesday showed some policy makers were open to raising rates if inflation remains sticky. The dollar and U.S. treasury yields rose in response.

Investors look ahead to key U.S. inflation and fourth-quarter growth prints due out Friday.

  • Oil prices extended the previous session's gains as the market grows increasingly worried about the potential for imminent U.S. action against Iran. Brent crude rose 0.9% to $70.98 a barrel, while WTI was up 0.7% to $65 a barrel after climbing more than 4% on Wednesday. "With a deal looking increasingly difficult to reach, it also means it will be more challenging to find a route to de-escalation, especially following the US military build-up we have seen in the region," ING analysts said. "And if de-escalation is not possible, the key question will then be what type of action the U.S. takes and how Iran responds to this."
  • Gold prices climbed back above $5,000, as concerns around the U.S. and Iran boosted the metal's appeal as a safe haven. In early trading, New York futures rose 0.3% to $5,025.90 a troy ounce. "Speculative positions are lean, and fresh longs can return as structural drivers are still in place," ANZ Research analysts said. Meanwhile, silver futures rose 1.3% to $78.61 an ounce.
  • Futures tied to U.S. blue-chip indexes nudged down slightly as traders weigh hawkish Federal Open Market Committee meeting minutes. The Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.1% premarket, while the S&P 500 was near-flat, falling 0.03%. The tech-heavy Nasdaq was flat premarket, after tacking higher in after-hours trading. Magnificent seven tech stocks are broadly higher, with Microsoft and Oracle both gaining 0.6% premarket.
  • Asian markets were mostly higher on Thursday, with Hong Kong and mainland China remaining closed for the Lunar New Year holiday that began Monday. South Korea returned to action, with the Kospi rising 3.1% to top 5,600 for the first time. Technology stocks led the gains, with Samsung Electronics reaching an intraday high and closing nearly 5% higher. Japan's Nikkei 225 also advanced, finishing 0.6% higher. In Southeast Asia, Singapore's Straits Times Index climbed 1.2%, while Malaysia's benchmark KLCI added 0.6%.
  • European indexes fell at the open as investors pulled back from a suite of record highs hit Wednesday. France's CAC 40 falls 0.35% while the FTSE 100 dropped 0.25% in London after both indexes closed at record highs in the last session. Miners weighed on the U.K., with Rio Tinto down 3.2% after announcing falling profits. Airbus was down 5.2%, dragging both the CAC 40 and the DAX, with the German index slipping 0.4% as automakers fell and chemicals group BASF slid 2%. The FTSE MIB was down 0.3% in Milan, as shipbuilder Fincantieri dropped 8%. Spain's IBEX 35 fell 0.1% as utilities energy companies were down, led by a 2.4% slip for Acciona Energia.
  • The dollar traded near a two-week high following the FOMC meeting minutes release. "The mere suggestion that the key interest rate could rise again is obviously making some market participants sit up and take notice," Commerzbank's Antje Praefcke said in a note. A rate cut in March appears unlikely and the market isn't even fully pricing in two rate cuts this year, she said. Friday's U.S. PCE inflation and fourth quarter U.S. economic growth data could boost the dollar further if they exceed expectations, she said. The DXY dollar index traded flat at 97.704, close to an earlier high of 97.777.
  • U.S. Treasury yields rose steeply in Asian trade. FOMC members anticipate inflation will move toward the 2% target but see risks to the inflation projection again as 'skewed to the upside'. The FOMC minutes "showed officials remained surprisingly cautious about cutting rates, with several even noting that another hike could be warranted if inflation proves sticky," Danske Bank's Kirstine Kundby-Nielsen said in a note. Danske continues to expect the Fed to deliver two more 25-basis-point cuts in June and September. The two-year Treasury yield was up 1.6 basis points at 3.475%, while the 10-year yield rose 2.3 bps to 4.103%, according to Tradeweb.

Eurozone government bond yields rose in early trade, matching moves in U.S. Treasurys. Thursday will see Spanish and French government bond auctions. Preliminary purchasing manager data from France, Germany and the eurozone are due Friday, followed by U.S. GDP and PCE inflation data. The 10-year German Bund yield rose 1.6 bps to 2.758%, according to LSEG.

  • Bitcoin rose modestly after gains in U.S. equities overnight. Bitcoin was up 1.4% to $67,202, while Ether gained 2.1% at $1,981.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com
